titleCard.title: The Phone Call Businesses Never Answer
titleCard.thesis: AI phone agents: the missed-call business, at every scale.

reveals.title: The install
reveals.body: AI receptionist · Plumbers · Dentists · Salons · Florists

reveals.title: Nobody's picking up
reveals.body: Rooftop technician · Dental chair · Flower shop counter

reveals.title: Good enough + cheap enough
reveals.body: The last two years

reveals.title: One person, end to end
reveals.body: No call center · No dev team

reveals.title: Four properties, Yucatán
reveals.body: Checkout rush · Phone under the queue

reveals.title: Bookings we never saw
reveals.body: A booking site · Or the hotel down the road

custom.quote: This voice? That's an AI voice.

reveals.title: If you didn't notice, that's the point.

custom.quote: If you didn't notice... that's the point.

screens.heading: The thesis

reveals.title: How good 'good enough' is
reveals.body: And where the seams still show

screens.source: Direct demonstration — this episode's own VO

reveals.title: Built lean
reveals.body: Laptop · Phone number · 3 subscriptions <$150/mo

reveals.title: Built seriously
reveals.body: 5–8 clients on retainer · Documented before/after · A demo that closes

risk.title: No verified solo case study exists.
risk.body: The low-end evidence is thinner than other AI-agency niches — and most of it comes from the platforms marketing to would-be resellers.
bullets: Treat reseller income claims as marketing artifacts
The absence is also why the space is less crowded

screens.heading: The evidence

reveals.title: Setup: $500–2,000
reveals.body: Marketing numbers — unverified

figure.source: Trillet pricing guide — vendor content, unverified

screens.source: Trillet pricing guide — vendor content, unverified

reveals.title: $500/mo against that is a rounding error
80% won't leave a message
reveals.body: Reported, not audited

reveals.title: ElevenLabs: $500M round · $11B valuation
reveals.body: ~$350M → ~$500M ARR in four months

reveals.title: Vapi: $500M valuation
reveals.body: Amazon Ring chose it over 40 rivals

reveals.title: Retell: 50M+ AI calls/month
reveals.body: Launched 2024 — vendor-reported

screens.heading: The evidence

reveals.title: Market: ~$2.5–3.5B, growing 30–40%/yr
reveals.body: Analyst estimate — directional

reveals.title: The read
reveals.body: Platforms raise at software multiples → someone installs → the dentist's office

screens.heading: The stack

reveals.title: ElevenLabs — the voice
reveals.body: The same voice you're hearing right now

reveals.title: Close to human on clean audio
reveals.body: Still stumbles: proper nouns, heavy accents

screens.source: Channel's own production stack — direct demonstration

reveals.title: Retell AI or Vapi — orchestration
reveals.body: Answer · Qualify · Book — ~7–15¢/min

reveals.title: Bland — outbound alternative
reveals.body: Raised per-minute prices, Dec 2025

reveals.title: You don't control this cost line.

custom.quote: You don't control this cost line. Nobody does.

reveals.title: Twilio — the number
reveals.body: ~$1/mo + usage

reveals.title: Make/n8n → Cal.com
reveals.body: Owner sees a booked slot, not a transcript

reveals.title: Solo stack: under $150/mo
reveals.body: Usage: tens of dollars, not hundreds — that gap is the margin

reveals.title: The delivery loop
reveals.body: Answer → Qualify → Book → Text the owner

reveals.title: The retainer buys maintenance
reveals.body: Prompts · FAQs · Integrations — the moat and the treadmill

reveals.title: So how do you land the first client?
Week one: pick ONE vertical
reveals.body: Home services · Dental · Salons · Florists — where a missed call is a missed job

reveals.title: 1,500+ florists, every Valentine's Day
reveals.body: Shop slammed · Phone ringing off the hook

reveals.title: The overflow goes to the 1-800 competitor
reveals.body: Every year. Same story.

reveals.title: Build the demo on a REAL business
reveals.body: Ring their missed-call line · Agent answers · Books a fake appointment

reveals.title: That demo is the sales call.

custom.quote: That demo is the sales call.

reveals.title: Their own phone, working correctly
reveals.body: For the first time

reveals.title: Receptionist replacement, never software
reveals.body: Setup: $500–2,000 · Retainer: $300–700/mo flat

reveals.title: Never quote per-minute
reveals.body: That's your cost structure, not theirs

reveals.title: Month one: 3 clients from your network
reveals.body: Their before/after missed-call numbers = the case study for the next five

risk.title: Outbound AI calling is TCPA territory.
risk.body: Inbound answering keeps you out of it. This is not the boundary to test as a solo operator.
bullets: Outbound = regulated, lawyered, not worth it at this scale

reveals.title: Month two: delivery discipline
reveals.body: Escalation path set BEFORE the first live call

reveals.title: Make the maintenance visible
reveals.body: Monthly report: calls answered · booked · revenue recovered — it renews the retainer

reveals.title: FAQ list from real transcripts, weekly
reveals.body: Better every week — or quietly worse

reveals.title: The blueprint — free, linked below
reveals.body: Vertical picker · Demo script · Pricing sheet

screens.heading: The economics

reveals.title: Year one: $1,500–5,000/mo
reveals.body: 3–8 local clients

reveals.title: An estimate, not audited results
reveals.body: Lower ceiling — thinner evidence, younger space

figure.text: Realistic year-one income by client count
figure.source: Estimate — reasoned from pricing bands, not audited

screens.source: Estimate — reasoned from pricing bands, not audited

reveals.title: 80% handled clean · 20% must escalate
reveals.body: Churn happens when escalation doesn't exist

risk.title: The failure mode isn't the pitch.
risk.body: It's the first embarrassing call — an angry customer, a mispronounced name, an accent the agent can't parse.
bullets: The other 20% must escalate to a human
Churn happens exactly when that path doesn't exist

screens.heading: The economics

reveals.title: You're reselling someone else's margin
reveals.body: Bland already raised prices once

reveals.title: Retell sells direct to SMBs already
reveals.body: The platforms could squeeze the agency layer out

screens.source: Bland pricing change, Dec 2025; Retell direct sales — vendor pages

risk.title: Platform risk is higher here.
risk.body: You're reselling someone else's per-minute margin, and the platforms are moving toward the same SMBs you serve.
bullets: Bland raised per-minute prices in Dec 2025
Retell already sells an AI receptionist direct
The agency layer could get squeezed

reveals.title: The open question
reveals.body: Not whether SMBs pay — whether the agency layer survives 18 months

reveals.title: …or the platforms just sell it themselves.

custom.quote: Or ElevenLabs and Retell just... sell it themselves.

reveals.title: № 002 — The AI Phone Agency
reveals.body: Vertical picker · Demo script, step by step · Pricing sheet (setup + retainer) · Every number sourced or flagged
